title: hiraea cephalotes
intro: Hiraea cephalotes, also known as the 'Tawa', is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and berries have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial and fungal infections. In herbal preparations, Hiraea cephalotes can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
